archetypes.name: Ball Running Fullback
archetypes.description: Fullbacks who are quick and able to break the defensive line, and opt for game breaking runs over tough carries.

archetypes.name: Balanced Fullback
archetypes.description: These well rounded fullbacks balance workrate, playmaking and elusiveness making them the complete package.

archetypes.name: Workhorse Fullback
archetypes.description: High-effort players who are always around the ball. They rack up high run metres and support plays.

archetypes.name: Playmaker Fullback
archetypes.description: These playmakers save their energy for the big moments, with reduced workrates but high involvement in tries and try assists.

archetypes.name: Support Fullback
archetypes.description: Players who are less involved in attack, but may specialise in defense or defusing kicks.

archetypes.name: Support Winger
archetypes.description: These wingers tend to be less involved in the game, perhaps due to lack of skill or opportunity.

archetypes.name: Finisher Winger
archetypes.description: Wingers who are specialist try scorers, often with great positional awareness and speed.

archetypes.name: Workhorse Winger
archetypes.description: High involvement wingers who are strong in contact, often taking carries out of their own end.

archetypes.name: Link Centre
archetypes.description: These centres play more of a Five-Eighth role with a high pass to run ratio, often looking to set up their winger.

archetypes.name: Workhorse Centre
archetypes.description: Attacking weapons who are heavily involved in gaining metres aswell as breaking the line and scoring tries.

archetypes.name: Support Centre
archetypes.description: These players are less involved with ball in hand and may play other roles for the team.

archetypes.name: Strike Centre
archetypes.description: Centres who are heavily involved in try scoring, and may look to set up those around them rather than taking tough carries.

archetypes.name: Dominant Half
archetypes.description: These players control the attack, and are usually relied upon to set up tries and do most of the kicking.

archetypes.name: Running Half
archetypes.description: Halves with strong running games who look to break the line, usually Five-Eighths.

archetypes.name: Organising Half
archetypes.description: Less dominant halves who may rely on their halves partner to control the attack, focusing on organising their edge.

archetypes.name: Balanced Hooker
archetypes.description: Hookers who balance dummy half runs and creativity.

archetypes.name: Running Hooker
archetypes.description: Strong ball running hookers who often look to run from dummy half.

archetypes.name: Link Hooker
archetypes.description: Hookers that look to pass rather than run, usually having strong ball playing.

archetypes.name: Crafty Hooker
archetypes.description: Creative types who specialise in finding the right pass for their forwards.

archetypes.name: Defensive Enforcer Edge
archetypes.description: Defensive specialists who are key in protecting their edge. Less involved in attacking situations.

archetypes.name: Support Edge
archetypes.description: These edges are less involved in attack and defense, and may specialise in other areas.

archetypes.name: Strong Attacking Edge
archetypes.description: These players are strong in contact and are relied upon to make metres for their team, often involved in tries as a result.

archetypes.name: Strike Attacking Edge
archetypes.description: Great line runners, often breaking the line and scoring tries, playing like a centre in attack.

archetypes.name: Ball Playing Middle
archetypes.description: These middles often play in the lock position with strong ball playing skills, directing players in the middle of the park.

archetypes.name: Impact Middle
archetypes.description: The most effective hit up takers, these middles are characterised by their strength and big engines.

archetypes.name: Standard Middle
archetypes.description: Making up the rest of the middle, these players share the hit up and tackling duties.
